Implement the following plan:

# PRO-101: Fix Permission Error on Personal Trainer Profile Access

## Context

Users receive a 403 error ("Você não tem permissão para acessar este recurso") when trying to view/search personal trainers. The error comes from the **CMS API** (REST), not do Firestore.

**Root cause**: O `CMSTrainerService.buildRequest()` envia SEMPRE um header `Authorization: Bearer {token}` em todas as requests — inclusive nos endpoints públicos como `GET /api/trainers`. Quando o Firebase ID token é inválido, expirado, ou não reconhecido pelo middleware de auth do CMS, o backend retorna HTTP 403. Porém, esses endpoints funcionam sem auth (verificado: `GET /api/trainers` retorna 200 sem header).

## Approach

Adicionar um parâmetro `requiresAuth` ao `buildRequest()` para que endpoints públicos (read-only) não enviem o header de autenticação. Endpoints que requerem autenticação (connect, reviews) continuam enviando.

## Changes

### 1. `CMSTrainerService.swift` (arquivo principal)

**Path**: `FitToday/FitToday/Data/Services/CMS/CMSTrainerService.swift`

Modificar `buildRequest()` (linha 165):

```swift
private func buildRequest(url: URL, method: String, requiresAuth: Bool = false) async throws -> URLRequest {
    var request = URLRequest(url: url)
    request.httpMethod = method
    request.setValue("application/json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Content-Type")
    request.setValue("application/json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Accept")

    if requiresAuth {
        if let tokenProvider {
            let token = try await tokenProvider()
            request.setValue("Bearer \(token)", forHTTPHeaderField: "Authorization")
        } else if let apiKey = configuration.apiKey {
            request.setValue("Bearer \(apiKey)", forHTTPHeaderField: "Authorization")
        }
    }

    return request
}
```

Marcar endpoints que necessitam auth com `requiresAuth: true`:

- `requestConnection()` (linha 101) — POST, precisa de auth
- `checkConnectionStatus()` (linha 116) — GET, precisa de auth (dados do aluno)
- `submitReview()` (linha 129) — POST, precisa de auth

Endpoints públicos ficam com default `false` (sem mudança nas chamadas):

- `fetchTrainers()` — lista pública do marketplace
- `fetchTrainerCount()` — contagem pública
- `fetchTrainer(id:)` — perfil público
- `fetchTrainerReviews()` — reviews públicos
- `fetchPrograms()` / `fetchProgram(id:)` — programas públicos

### 2. Nenhuma outra mudança necessária

- **Firestore rules**: Corretas (`allow read: if isAuthenticated()` para trainers)
- **DI Container**: Não precisa mudar
- **ViewModels/Views**: Erro já é tratado via `.alert`
- **Use Cases**: Sem mudança

## Verification

1. Buildar o app e abrir a tela de Personal Trainer
2. Verificar que a listagem de trainers carrega sem erro 403
3. Verificar que a busca por nome funciona
4. Verificar que busca por invite code funciona
5. Verificar que o botão "Conectar" ainda funciona (requer auth)
6. Testar com usuário free e pro — ambos devem ver trainers
7. Rodar tests existentes: `EntitlementPolicyTests`, `PersonalTrainerViewModelTests`


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/viniciuscarvalho/.REDACTED.jsonl

# PRO-101: Fix Permission Error on Personal Trainer Profile Access

## Context

Users receive a 403 error ("Você não tem permissão para acessar este recurso") when trying to view/search personal trainers. The error comes from the **CMS API** (REST), not do Firestore.

**Root cause**: O `CMSTrainerService.buildRequest()` envia SEMPRE um header `Authorization: Bearer {token}` em todas as requests — inclusive nos endpoints públicos como `GET /api/trainers`. Quando o Firebase ID token é inválido, expirado, ou não reconhecido pelo middleware de auth do CMS, o backend retorna HTTP 403. Porém, esses endpoints funcionam sem auth (verificado: `GET /api/trainers` retorna 200 sem header).

## Approach

Adicionar um parâmetro `requiresAuth` ao `buildRequest()` para que endpoints públicos (read-only) não enviem o header de autenticação. Endpoints que requerem autenticação (connect, reviews) continuam enviando.

## Changes

### 1. `CMSTrainerService.swift` (arquivo principal)

**Path**: `FitToday/FitToday/Data/Services/CMS/CMSTrainerService.swift`

Modificar `buildRequest()` (linha 165):

```swift
private func buildRequest(url: URL, method: String, requiresAuth: Bool = false) async throws -> URLRequest {
    var request = URLRequest(url: url)
    request.httpMethod = method
    request.setValue("application/json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Content-Type")
    request.setValue("application/json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Accept")

    if requiresAuth {
        if let tokenProvider {
            let token = try await tokenProvider()
            request.setValue("Bearer \(token)", forHTTPHeaderField: "Authorization")
        } else if let apiKey = configuration.apiKey {
            request.setValue("Bearer \(apiKey)", forHTTPHeaderField: "Authorization")
        }
    }

    return request
}
```

Marcar endpoints que necessitam auth com `requiresAuth: true`:

- `requestConnection()` (linha 101) — POST, precisa de auth
- `checkConnectionStatus()` (linha 116) — GET, precisa de auth (dados do aluno)
- `submitReview()` (linha 129) — POST, precisa de auth

Endpoints públicos ficam com default `false` (sem mudança nas chamadas):

- `fetchTrainers()` — lista pública do marketplace
- `fetchTrainerCount()` — contagem pública
- `fetchTrainer(id:)` — perfil público
- `fetchTrainerReviews()` — reviews públicos
- `fetchPrograms()` / `fetchProgram(id:)` — programas públicos

### 2. Nenhuma outra mudança necessária

- **Firestore rules**: Corretas (`allow read: if isAuthenticated()` para trainers)
- **DI Container**: Não precisa mudar
- **ViewModels/Views**: Erro já é tratado via `.alert`
- **Use Cases**: Sem mudança

## Verification

1. Buildar o app e abrir a tela de Personal Trainer
2. Verificar que a listagem de trainers carrega sem erro 403
3. Verificar que a busca por nome funciona
4. Verificar que busca por invite code funciona
5. Verificar que o botão "Conectar" ainda funciona (requer auth)
6. Testar com usuário free e pro — ambos devem ver trainers
7. Rodar tests existentes: `EntitlementPolicyTests`, `PersonalTrainerViewModelTests`

   194→        #endif
   195→

   200→            } catch {

   202→                print("[CMSTrainerService] Decode error: \(error)")

   206→                #endif
   207→                throw CMSServiceError.decodingFailed(error)
   208→            }
   209→        case 401: throw CMSServiceError.unauthorized

   211→        case 404: throw CMSServiceError.notFound
   212→        case 429: throw CMSServiceError.rateLimited
   213→        case 500...599: throw CMSServiceError.serverError(httpResponse.statusCode)

   217→            }
   218→            throw CMSServiceError.unexpectedStatus(httpResponse.statusCode)

All three changes are done. The SourceKit diagnostics are just IDE noise from types defined in other files — not actual build errors.

Here's a summary of the changes to `CMSTrainerService.swift`:

1. **`buildRequest()`** — Added `requiresAuth: Bool = false` parameter. Auth headers are now only sent when `requiresAuth` is `true`.

2. **Three endpoints marked with `requiresAuth: true`**:
   - `requestConnection()` (POST) — line 101
   - `checkConnectionStatus()` (GET) — line 116  
   - `submitReview()` (POST) — line 129

3. **Six public endpoints remain with default `false`** (no auth header sent):
   - `fetchTrainers()`, `fetchTrainerCount()`, `fetchTrainer(id:)`, `fetchTrainerReviews()`, `fetchPrograms()`, `fetchProgram(id:)`

This ensures public read-only endpoints won't trigger 403 errors from expired/invalid tokens, while authenticated endpoints continue sending the Bearer token as before.

content.text:
Found it. The `toDictionary()` method (used for new users) **never writes a `role` field** to Firestore. The CMS backend checks `userData?.role === 'student'` (line 26 of the connect route), but the iOS app never sets `role` on user documents.

The fix is to include `"role": "student"` in the user dictionary when saving to Firestore. All iOS app users are students.

content.text:
Here's a summary of all the changes made:

**Root cause**: The CMS backend's `/api/trainers/[id]/connect` endpoint checks `authResult.role === 'student'` and returns 403 if the role doesn't match. The iOS app never wrote a `role` field to the user's Firestore document, so `role` was `null`.

**Changes made (3 files)**:

1. **`CMSTrainerService.swift`** — Added `requiresAuth` parameter to `buildRequest()`. Public endpoints (list, count, profile, reviews, programs) don't send auth headers. Only `requestConnection`, `checkConnectionStatus`, and `submitReview` send auth.

2. **`SocialUserMapper.swift`** — Added `"role": "student"` to `toDictionary()` so new users get the role field in Firestore.

3. **`FirebaseAuthService.swift`** — Added `"role": "student"` to the merge update for existing users, so they get the role field on their next sign-in.

## Summary

- **Public endpoints no longer send auth headers**: Added `requiresAuth` parameter to `CMSTrainerService.buildRequest()`. Public read-only endpoints (list trainers, count, profile, reviews, programs) no longer send `Authorization: Bearer` headers, preventing 403 errors from expired/invalid tokens.
- **Student role ensured before connecting**: CMS backend requires `role: "student"` on the user's Firestore document. Added `ensureStudentRole()` that calls `POST /api/users/me` before the connect request, safely setting the role via the CMS API (with role validation and downgrade protection for trainers).
- **Three authenticated endpoints marked**: `requestConnection`, `checkConnectionStatus`, and `submitReview` correctly send auth headers.

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `CMSTrainerService.swift` | Added `requiresAuth` param to `buildRequest()`, added `ensureStudentRole()` method |
| `CMSTrainerModels.swift` | Added `CMSUserProfileResponse` DTO |
| `FirebasePersonalTrainerRepository.swift` | Calls `ensureStudentRole()` before `requestConnection()` |

## Root Causes Found

1. `buildRequest()` always sent auth headers → public endpoints failed with 403 when token was expired
2. CMS `/connect` endpoint checks `authResult.role === 'student'` → user documents had no `role` field → 403

## Test Plan

- [x] Build succeeds on iOS Simulator (iPhone 17 Pro)
- [ ] Open Personal Trainer screen — listing loads without 403

- [ ] EntitlementPolicyTests pass (blocked by pre-existing API signature mismatch: `isPro` → `tier`)

> **Note**: Test target has pre-existing compilation errors in `EntitlementPolicyTests`, `WorkoutVariationValidatorTests`, and `SaveCustomWorkoutUseCaseTests` unrelated to this PR. PersonalTrainerViewModelTests has no compilation errors.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

content.text:
PR created: https://github.com/Viniciuscarvalho/FitToday/pull/107

**Summary of all changes:**

1. **`CMSTrainerService.swift`** — Added `requiresAuth: Bool = false` to `buildRequest()`. Public endpoints don't send auth. Three endpoints (`requestConnection`, `checkConnectionStatus`, `submitReview`) marked with `requiresAuth: true`. Added `ensureStudentRole()` that calls `POST /api/users/me`.

2. **`CMSTrainerModels.swift`** — Added `CMSUserProfileResponse` DTO for the `/api/users/me` response.

3. **`FirebasePersonalTrainerRepository.swift`** — Calls `ensureStudentRole(displayName:)` before `requestConnection()` to ensure the user has `role: "student"` in the CMS.

**Build status**: Main app target builds successfully. Test target has pre-existing compilation errors in unrelated test files (`EntitlementPolicyTests`, `WorkoutVariationValidatorTests`). `PersonalTrainerViewModelTests` has zero compilation errors.

**Manual verification needed**: Open the app, test trainer listing, search, and the connect flow with both free and pro users.

Fiz clean build. Roda o app novamente no simulador (Product > Run no Xcode, ou `Cmd+R`) e tenta conectar. O log agora deve mostrar:

1. `POST .../api/users/me` — chamada do `ensureStudentRole()`

3. `POST .../connect` — chamada de conexão

Se o `POST /api/users/me` **não aparecer**, significa que o Xcode não rodou a build nova. Nesse caso, faça `Cmd+Shift+K` (Clean Build Folder) no Xcode e rode novamente com `Cmd+R`.
